spread_sign_convention: spread = DA - RT; positive => DA expensive => Short DA / Long RT signal
confidence_discount_reason: Smartbidder DA-RT P file fully available (2nd consecutive cycle after 6-day gap). Full 24-hour P(DA>RT) and P(DA<RT) populated. Raising from 0.85 (prior cycle) toward target 1.00 over 3 validated cycles.
leakage_check: COMPLIANT — all sources pre 2026-05-27 10:00 CT DAM cutoff

key_market_inputs.south_houston_import_p_binding_pct: 20-28% (MEDIUM, Stage 0 heuristic — downgraded from prior cycle due to stronger evening wind 8321 MW)
key_market_inputs.oversupply_event_he09_13_probability_pct: 50-60% (HIGH — NL trough 29700 MW, solar 30118 MW, wind 727 MW)

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=55.5%, spread DA-RT +$1.93 (with 1.20x bias correction). Solar HE10 peak window (Enverus NL 29700 MW trough). Oversupply event (50-60% probability) would push RT lower, widening Short DA spread. WEST_TO_NORTH binding (30-40%) risks HB_NORTH RT rise — adversarial scenario. MED sizing (25 MW) reflects thin spread and balanced congestion risk.
positions.risk_note: WEST_TO_NORTH binding would raise HB_NORTH RT above DA, reversing Short DA to a loss. 30-40% binding probability is material. Max adverse: 25 MW x $1.93 = -$48. Oversupply scenario (RT negative) is favorable.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=57.1%. Solar near peak, Enverus wind 1134 MW at HE11 (extremely low). Direction reversal from prior cycles (was LONG DA when RT > DA): today Smartbidder forecasts DA marginally above RT even at solar peak hour. Oversupply supports Short DA thesis — if RT goes to $0, spread +$23 is a large win. MED 25 MW.
positions.risk_note: Same WEST_TO_NORTH constraint risk as HE10. Max loss: 25 MW x $1.51 = -$38. Prior cycle used LONG DA at this hour — direction reversal needs validation.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=58.4% — highest in midday block. Solar Enverus peak 30118 MW at HE13. DA $26.55 still solar-suppressed but above RT $25.46. Load ramp beginning HE14+ will alleviate West surplus — this is the terminal hour of the oversupply window. MED 25 MW as signal is attenuating.
positions.risk_note: If WEST_TO_NORTH binds intensely at HE13, HB_NORTH RT could spike above DA. Also: Enverus solar 30118 MW vs Yes Energy 26822 MW — if realized lower, NL higher and oversupply less extreme.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=61.9% crosses HIGH threshold but this is a new entry hour not validated in prior cycles. DA $33.54 vs RT $30.19 — cooling load ramp pushes DA above RT in afternoon ramp window. MED 25 MW for first exploration of afternoon Short DA block. If validated across 2-3 cycles, eligible for HIGH upgrade.
positions.risk_note: Afternoon hour with no prior position history. DA-RT spread narrows if RT demand surge is stronger than DAM priced. Sized at MED to limit exposure during validation.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=65.3% — direct file read, HIGH tier. DA $43.52 vs RT $34.93 — Evening Ramp transition. Solar HE19 Enverus 8448 MW declining sharply. Net load 54816 MW rising steeply. SOUTH/HOUSTON IMPORT constraint opening window (20-28% binding probability). Validated entry hour over 4+ prior cycles. HIGH 50 MW.
positions.risk_note: Transition hour: solar still partially present, ramp not fully established. RT could spike if load surprises upward. SOUTH/HOUSTON IMPORT binding (20-28%) would raise HB_HOUSTON RT — but P(DA>RT)=65.3% already prices this risk.

positions.rationale: PRIMARY TRADE. Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=68.4% — highest in book. DA $79.48 vs RT $45.21, spread +$34.27 raw (>$41 bias-corrected). NL 58738 MW (Enverus HE20) — daily peak. Solar 580 MW (near zero). Evening Ramp complete. DAM priced in scarcity premium; Smartbidder RT forecast $45.21 reflects evening wind recovery (8321 MW Enverus HE20) providing meaningful RT cushion. SOUTH/HOUSTON IMPORT binding adds basis upside. This is the defining trade of the cycle.
positions.risk_note: ORDC tail risk: cap-out ~16900 MW + NL 58738 MW = narrow reserve margin. Unplanned outage could drive 5-min RT above $79.48. This is the highest dollar risk position — if SHORT DA loses here, full spread reversal = 50 MW x $41 = -$2,056. Evening wind at 8321 MW is the primary RT suppressor and main risk mitigant.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=67.5% but P(DA<RT)=49.8% — bimodal distribution at DA $89.18. DA $89.18 is extreme; bid price capped at $75.00 per established risk rule (HE21 extreme DA price cap protocol, first applied 2026-05-25 self-review). Effective E[spread] = $75 - $55.05 = $19.95 (x1.20 bias = $23.94). If RT settles above $75, position is a loss — buffer vs DA $89.18 is $14.18. Net Load 55428 MW (HE21 Enverus), declining from HE20 peak. Wind recovery 9947 MW at HE21 supports RT suppression. HIGH 50 MW with cap discipline.
positions.risk_note: CRITICAL: P(DA<RT)=49.8% means ~50% probability RT exceeds Smartbidder forecast DA. If RT spikes above $75 (cap), position loses. Spike scenario: ORDC trigger at HE20 carries into HE21. Max loss at $75 cap: 50 MW x ($75 - realized_RT if >$75). Monitor HE20 settlement as leading indicator before HE21.

positions.rationale: Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=66.5% — clean HIGH tier. DA $50.05 vs RT $43.27. Wind building (Enverus HE22 ~10.8 GW) suppresses RT below DA. Normal weekday HE22 HIGH 50 MW standard tier — established 2026-05-24 self-review, confirmed across 3 subsequent cycles. SOUTH/HOUSTON IMPORT constraint easing, basis narrowing but still positive.
positions.risk_note: HE21 RT spike spillover risk: if HE21 RT elevated, HE22 RT may remain elevated. Wind recovery is primary risk mitigant. P(DA>RT)=66.5% is robust — minor spike bleed from HE21 unlikely to flip direction.

positions.rationale: NEW ENTRY HOUR. Smartbidder P(DA>RT)=62.4% — above HIGH threshold but first time entering HE23. DA $40.31 vs RT $35.41, spread $4.90 raw. Prior cycles always skipped HE23-24 due to weak signal; today P is clearly above 60% with meaningful spread. Wind HE23 Enverus 10836 MW — strong but DA $40 is still significantly elevated. MED 25 MW cautious entry. Validate over 2-3 cycles before HIGH upgrade.
positions.risk_note: Overnight hour with no prior position history. Wind recovery speed could be faster than Enverus 10836 MW — if RT drops faster, spread could narrow. Sized at MED (25 MW) to limit exposure while establishing track record.

skipped_hours.HE01-06: Long DA signal (P(DA<RT) 0.617-0.674) but: (1) No settlement history for overnight DART virtual positions in any prior cycle. (2) DA prices $22-31, thin absolute spread ($0.46-4.13). (3) Overnight liquidity constraints. Maintain skip rule until overnight position track record established.

skipped_hours.HE09: Short DA direction. P(DA>RT)=0.585, below 60% threshold. Skip by matrix rule.
skipped_hours.HE12: Short DA direction. P(DA>RT)=0.551 — barely above 55% MED floor. Corrected spread $1.55 yields EV ~$4 at 25 MW. Too thin to justify position. Skip.
skipped_hours.HE14-15: Short DA direction. P(DA>RT) 0.618-0.619 — crosses HIGH threshold but raw spread only $0.70-1.79, bias-corrected $0.84-2.15. E[PnL] $10-26 at 50 MW. Too thin for capital allocation. Skip.
skipped_hours.HE17: Short DA direction. P(DA>RT)=0.606. Spread $1.17 (corrected $1.40). E[PnL] $15 at 50 MW. Too thin. Skip.
skipped_hours.HE18: Ramp onset RT spike asymmetric risk. Rule preserved across all prior cycles — solar 20206 MW still present at HE18 (Enverus), load approaching 73485 MW peak, transition to evening regime creates RT spike risk. Skip maintained.
skipped_hours.HE24: Short DA direction. P(DA>RT)=0.617. Raw spread $0.72 (corrected $0.86). E[PnL] $10 at 50 MW. Negligible. Skip.

summary.strategy_note: Full Short DA day — direction reversal from prior cycles in midday block (HE10-13 was LONG DA, now SHORT DA as Smartbidder shows DA>RT). Evening block unchanged structure. HE23 new entry. HE21 DA $89.18 capped at $75.00 per established bid cap protocol.
